_note: Fictional regulatory and market news feed. 15 invented items over the past 6 weeks. Helpful for client briefings, knowledge bulletins and 'what changed this week' use cases.

news.headline: ICO publishes updated guidance on cookie consent platforms
news.summary: Updated guidance clarifies that 'accept all' and 'reject all' must be equally prominent and that consent must be specific to processing purposes. Cookie walls remain non-compliant. Bulletin from the firm's Data Protection PSL.

news.headline: Procurement Review Unit issues first three section 108 recommendations
news.summary: The new Procurement Review Unit, established under the Procurement Act 2023, has issued its first three section 108 recommendations. Two relate to insufficient market engagement; one to disproportionate exclusion grounds.

news.headline: FCA finalises rules on AI use in retail insurance
news.summary: FCA publishes final rules requiring (i) explicit board approval of any AI tool that materially affects retail decisions, (ii) periodic outcome testing for fairness, (iii) explainability documentation accessible to consumers on request. In force from 1 September 2026.

news.headline: Court of Appeal: AI-generated trade mark applications must show genuine human input
news.summary: Court holds that trade mark applications generated entirely by automated systems are vulnerable to revocation absent demonstrable human creative input. Case sent back to UKIPO for reconsideration. Likely to influence brand-clearance practice.

news.headline: HMRC consults on R&D tax credit reforms (third round)
news.summary: Third consultation on R&D tax credits proposes a unified scheme, tighter oversight of advisor accreditation, and a new requirement for PAYE-linked salary backing for SME claimants. Closes 18 July 2026.

news.headline: Employment Tribunal: long Covid is a disability for purposes of the Equality Act
news.summary: Tribunal finds long Covid satisfied the disability test where impairment had lasted 14+ months and substantially affected day-to-day activities. Reasonable adjustments duty engaged. Practitioner note: limited persuasive value but useful for triage.

news.headline: Bank of England raises bank rate to 4.50% (split decision)
news.summary: Five to four vote in favour of a 25bp hike to 4.50%. Markets had priced in a hold. Floating-rate borrowers should expect sharper margin step-ups; expect renewed focus on MAC and prepayment provisions.

news.headline: ASA upholds 12 complaints against AI-generated celebrity endorsement ads
news.summary: Advertising Standards Authority has upheld 12 complaints against synthetic-celebrity endorsement adverts circulated on social media. Industry guidance imminent.

news.headline: ICO opens consultation on AI in employment decisions
news.summary: Consultation focuses on automated decision-making in hiring, performance management and termination. Closes 30 June 2026. Likely to feed into FCA and EHRC guidance later in the year.

news.headline: FCA Dear CEO letter on consumer duty failures in motor finance commission redress
news.summary: Letter directs firms to issue interim updates to affected customers within 90 days of decision and to ringfence operational capacity for redress administration. Affects c.27 firms.

news.headline: Supreme Court: implied indemnity in standard supply terms cannot extend to indirect losses
news.summary: Supreme Court reverses CoA. Implied indemnity in standard B2B supply terms held not to extend to consequential or indirect losses absent clear words. Important for boilerplate review of legacy supply contracts.

news.headline: ICO fines a UK retailer £4.2m for cookie-consent violations
news.summary: ICO imposes its largest cookie-consent fine to date. Findings: (i) reject-all hidden behind two clicks, (ii) consent strings not refreshed for 22 months, (iii) third-party tags fired before any consent action. Decision sets the bar for materiality.

news.headline: TUPE: 'organised grouping of employees' includes ad-hoc project teams (CoA)
news.summary: Court of Appeal holds that an organised grouping for TUPE purposes can crystallise during a project even without pre-existing structure, where the team is dedicated to delivery of services to a specific client.

news.headline: Companies House launches mandatory ID verification for new directors
news.summary: Mandatory ID verification rolls out 1 September 2026. Existing directors have 12-month transition period. Practitioner note: brief clients now to avoid filing delays in busy autumn period.

news.headline: EU EU AI Act: first-tier obligations on high-risk systems take effect 2 August
news.summary: First obligations under the EU AI Act for high-risk systems take effect 2 August 2026. UK firms exporting to EU users should review classification and conformity assessment requirements.
